title: Plant Chromatin Immunoprecipitation
author: Laura Poza-Viejo
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.444gyyw

steps.title: Harvest 1-2 g of Arabidopsis or...
steps.instruction: Harvest 1-2 g of Arabidopsis or Brassica seedlings/leaf tissue/inflorescence in a 50ml conical centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add Phosphate-buffered saline buffer (PBS) with 1% formaldehyde (FAA) 1
steps.instruction: Add Phosphate-buffered saline buffer (PBS) with 1% formaldehyde (FAA) 1 .To detect histone modifications this is enough, but for protein binding we perform a dual crosslinking with DSG (disuccinimidyl glutarate) 2 mM.

steps.title: Stop the crosslinking by adding Glycine...
steps.instruction: Stop the crosslinking by adding Glycine 2 M to a final concentration of 0.125 M. (2.5 ml of 2 M Glycine in 37 ml of water), vacuum for 5 min.

steps.title: Remove the tissue from the vacuum...
steps.instruction: Remove the tissue from the vacuum and rinse off the formaldehyde with MilliQ water twice. Remove as much water as possible from the seedlings by placing on a paper towel.

steps.title: Freeze in liquid nitrogen and store crosslinked tissue at -80°C
steps.instruction: Freeze in liquid nitrogen and store crosslinked tissue at -80°C

steps.title: Grind with mortar in liquid nitrogen to a fine powder
steps.instruction: Grind with mortar in liquid nitrogen to a fine powder.

steps.title: Pass to a 50 ml centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Pass to a 50 ml centrifuge tube and add the powder to 10 ml of Extraction Buffer 1. After adding buffer, ensure you mix well so all the tissue has dissolved into the buffer.

steps.title: Filter the solution through a layer...
steps.instruction: Filter the solution through a layer of Miracloth into a 50ml centrifuge tube. Repeat step 9 until the solution is clear of big tissue particles.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5000 xg for 10 min at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5000 xg for 10 min at 4°C.

steps.title: Gently remove supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Gently remove supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 ml of Extraction Buffer 2. Transfer the solution to 1.5 ml centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5000 xg for 10 min at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5000 xg for 10 min at 4°C.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend pellet in...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and resuspend pellet in 300 µl of Extraction Buffer 3.

steps.title: In a clean eppendorf, add 600...
steps.instruction: In a clean eppendorf, add 600 µl of Extraction Buffer 3. Take the 300 µl solution (resuspended pellet) from step 13 and carefully lay it on top of the clean 600 µl of Extraction Buffer 3.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16000 xg for 60min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16000 xg for 60min at 4 °C. At this point, it is advisable to prepare Nuclei Lysis Buffer and ChIP Dilution Buffer, to turn on the Bioruptor and to prepare the Dynabeads (go to step 20).

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the chromatin pellet in 200 µl of Nuclei Lysis Buffer 3 .

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet by pipetting up...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet by pipetting up and down (keep solution cold). Take a 5 µl aliquot of each sample to check sonication efficiency 4 .

steps.title: Prepare the Dynabeads magnetic beads (Protein...
steps.instruction: Prepare the Dynabeads magnetic beads (Protein A for rabbit antibody or Protein G for mouse antibody). For each immunoprecipitation wash 15 µl Dynabeads in 500 µl ChIP dilution buffer twice. Then, add 1-5 µg of antibody and incubate the Dynabeads and the antibody in 50 µl ChIP Dilution Buffer rotating at 4°C for 1 h.

steps.title: Wash prepared antibody coated Dynabeads with...
steps.instruction: Wash prepared antibody coated Dynabeads with 500 µl of ChIP dilution buffer. Let Dynabeads attach to the magnet. Then, discard buffer. Resuspend Dynabeads in 50µl of ChIP Dilution Buffer and keep aside on ice.

steps.title: For the immunoprecipitation, measure chromatin solution...
steps.instruction: For the immunoprecipitation, measure chromatin solution from step 19, usually ~150 µl. Take INPUT: 10% of the volume (15 µl) of chromatin and store it at -20°C.

steps.title: Dilute chromatin solution 10 times adding 1
steps.instruction: Dilute chromatin solution 10 times adding 1.5 ml of ChIP Dilution Buffer. The point here is to dilute the 1% SDS to 0.1% SDS with ChIP dilution buffer. This is enough for two immunoprecipitations or one immunoprecipitation and no-antibody control sample. For three immunoprecipitations is better to start from 300 µl of Nuclei Lysis Buffer (step 16).

steps.title: Take the INPUTs frozen the day...
steps.instruction: Take the INPUTs frozen the day before and elute immune complexes and INPUTs by adding 200 µl 10% Chelex 100 resin (10 g in 100 ml water) and boil the samples at 98°C for 10 min with agitation in a thermomixer.

steps.title: Cool in ice and incubate with...
steps.instruction: Cool in ice and incubate with 2 µl Proteinase K (10 mg/ml) at 37°C for 20 min.

steps.title: Boil samples again at 98°C for...
steps.instruction: Boil samples again at 98°C for 10 min with agitation in thermomixer to inactivate Proteinase K.

steps.title: Cool in ice and spin the...
steps.instruction: Cool in ice and spin the tubes in microcentrifuge at 16000 xg for 5 min at 4°C. Transfer ~160 µl of the supernatant to a new DNA low binding microtube. Do not take any Chelex particle. Store at 4°C or at 20-°C for long term storage.

steps.title: For real-time quantitative PCR analysis, ChIP...
steps.instruction: For real-time quantitative PCR analysis, ChIP DNA can be used directly but INPUT DNA must be diluted at least 1:10 in water 7 . Usually 1-2 µl of the DNA solution is enough for PCR amplification.

steps.title: Crosslink in a desiccator attached to...
steps.instruction: Crosslink in a desiccator attached to a vaccum pump for 5 + 5 min (for Arabidopsis seedlings and inflorescences) or 5 min + 10 min + 10 min (for Brassica leaves and inflorescences) 2 .

steps.title: Add antibody coated Dynabeads into the...
steps.instruction: Add antibody coated Dynabeads into the 750 μl of chromatin solution in a DNA low binding microtube. Incubate the immunoprecipitation rotating at 4°C overnight 6 .

steps.title: The next morning perform the washes...
steps.instruction: The next morning perform the washes using 1 ml of each buffer (see below). Wash at 4°C for 5 min with gentle rotation and let beads attach to magnets in between washes. Use the sequence of buffers listed below: a) Low Salt Wash Buffer (2 washes) b) High Salt Wash Buffer (1 wash, optional) c) LiCl Wash Buffer (1 wash, optional) d) TE Buffer (2 washes)

steps.title: Prepare the Elution buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare the Elution buffer. Elute immune complexes by adding 50 μl of fresh Elution Buffer to the pelleted beads. Vortex briefly to mix and incubate at 65 °C for 15 min with gentle agitation. Let beads attach to magnet and carefully transfer 40 μl of the supernatant fraction (eluate) to another tube and repeat elution. Combine the two eluates (Final volume = 80 μl).

steps.title: Take the INPUTs frozen the day before
steps.instruction: Take the INPUTs frozen the day before. Add 3.2 μl 5 M NaCl to each ChIP eluate and to the INPUTs and reverse crosslink incubating overnight at 65 °C.

steps.title: Next day, add 180 µl (2
steps.instruction: Next day, add 180 µl (2.2x vol.) of SPRI beads. Carefully mix by pipetting up and down (at least ten times) and incubate for 5 min at room temperature. Separate supernatant from beads by placing the tubes in the magnet for 5 min. Discard supernatant but leave tubes on magnet.

steps.title: Elute DNA from SPRI beads in...
steps.instruction: Elute DNA from SPRI beads in 30 µl of Tris-HCl 10 mM pH 8. Mix by pipetting at least ten times and incubate for 2 min.

steps.title: Place tube on magnet and incubate...
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnet and incubate for 5 min, and finally transfer supernatant to a new tube (aprox. 25 µl). DNA concentratin can be determined using a Qubit fluorometer. Usually 1-10 µl are used for ChIP-seq library preparation.

steps.title: Wash SPRI beads with 100 µl...
steps.instruction: Wash SPRI beads with 100 µl of 70% ethanol incubating or 30 sec, without disturbing the beads, then discard supernatant. Repeat wahses four times.

steps.title: Move tubes off the magnet and...
steps.instruction: Move tubes off the magnet and air dry the SPRI beads for 4 min at room-temperature.
